steps.title: Identify the compound predicate structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: “The lock would increase the salinity of the lakes and ______ a natural barrier.” This is a compound predicate — two verbs joined by “and” that share the same subject, “The lock.” When verbs are connected this way, they must be parallel in form and tense.

steps.title: Match the tense to maintain parallelism
steps.content:
The first verb is “would increase” — a conditional form combining the modal “would” with the base verb “increase.” For parallelism, the second verb must follow the same pattern: “would [base verb].” Since the second “would” is implied after the conjunction, the verb must be the base form “create,” producing “would increase and create.”

Choices A, C, and D break this parallelism by using different forms: a present-tense verb (creates), a gerund (creating), and a past-tense verb (created), respectively.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
